message:
Improve mobile spacing, scroll animation triggers, and bottom nav coverage

app/architecture/page.tsx:
- Reduce header padding on mobile from pt-44/pb-20 to pt-32/pb-16
  (keeping desktop values via md: breakpoint). The original 44-unit
  top padding pushed the hero heading below the fold on most phones,
  wasting the critical first-screen impression.
- Tighten DecisionCard padding on mobile from p-8 to p-6 (md:p-10
  unchanged). Cards were too padded on narrow viewports, limiting
  visible content per card.
- Same treatment for the architectural rules grid cards: p-6 md:p-8
  instead of uniform p-8.

components/section-shell.tsx:
- Reduce vertical section padding across all breakpoints:
  py-16 (was py-24), md:py-32 (was md:py-40), lg:py-48 (was lg:py-56).
  The previous values created excessive whitespace between sections,
  making the page feel disconnected on scroll — especially on mobile
  where 6rem top+bottom padding consumed most of the viewport.
- Lower intersection observer threshold from 0.1 to 0.05 so section
  entrance animations trigger when just 5% of the section is visible
  rather than 10%. This makes content feel more responsive as the
  user scrolls, reducing the "nothing happening" dead zone.

components/timeline.tsx:
- Reduce viewport margin for whileInView animation from -100px to
  -20px. The -100px margin meant items didn't animate until they were
  100px past the viewport edge, creating a jarring delay. At -20px
  the animation fires almost immediately as items enter view.

components/site-header.tsx:
- Show 6 nav items in the mobile bottom tab bar instead of 4
  (navItems.slice(0, 6) vs slice(0, 4)). With only 4 items, key
  pages like Glossary and Getting Started were hidden behind the
  "More" drawer, requiring two taps to reach.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
